prompt: Implement minigit as described in SPEC-v1.txt using Ruby. The executable must be named 'minigit' and be runnable as ./minigit. For compiled languages, include a Makefile or build script. For interpreted languages, ensure the minigit file has a proper shebang line and is executable. Verify your implementation passes all tests by running: bash test-v1.sh
response:
```ruby
#!/usr/bin/env ruby
# minigit – a minimal Git‐like content‐addressable object store.

require 'digest/sha1'
require 'fileutils'
require 'time'

GIT_DIR = '.git'
OBJECTS_DIR = File.join(GIT_DIR, 'objects')
INDEX_FILE = File.join(GIT_DIR, 'index')

def die(msg)
  warn msg
  exit 1
end

def init
  FileUtils.mkdir_p(OBJECTS_DIR)
  puts "Initialized empty minigit repository"
end

def hash_object(path)
  die "hash-object requires a file path" unless path
  die "cannot hash a directory" if File.directory?(path)
  die "cannot open #{path}" unless File.exist?(path)

  content = File.binread(path)
  header = "blob #{content.bytesize}\0"
  store = header + content
  sha = Digest::SHA1.hexdigest(store)
  FileUtils.mkdir_p(OBJECTS_DIR)
  File.binwrite(File.join(OBJECTS_DIR, sha), store)
  puts sha
end

def cat_file(sha)
  die "cat-file requires a SHA" unless sha
  path = File.join(OBJECTS_DIR, sha)
  die "not a valid object name #{sha}" unless File.exist?(path)

  raw = File.binread(path)
  header, content = raw.split("\0", 2)
  print content
end

def update_index(*paths)
  die "update-index requires at least one path" if paths.empty?

  entries = []
  paths.each do |path|
    die "#{path} does not exist" unless File.exist?(path)
    die "#{path} is a directory" if File.directory?(path)
    content = File.binread(path)
    header = "blob #{content.bytesize}\0"
    store = header + content
    sha = Digest::SHA1.hexdigest(store)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(OBJECTS_DIR)
    File.binwrite(File.join(OBJECTS_DIR, sha), store)
    mtime = File.mtime(path).to_i
    entries << "#{sha} #{mtime} #{path}"
  end

  File.open(INDEX_FILE, 'a') do |f|
    entries.each { |e| f.puts e }
  end
end

def write_tree
  die "index does not exist" unless File.exist?(INDEX_FILE)

  tree_entries = []
  File.readlines(INDEX_FILE, chomp: true).each do |line|
    sha, mtime, path = line.split(' ', 3)
    tree_entries << "100644 blob #{sha}\t#{path}"
  end

  tree_content = +''
  tree_entries.sort.each do |entry|
    mode, type, sha, name = entry.split(/[\t ]/, 4)
    tree_content << "#{mode} #{type} #{sha}\t#{name}\n"
  end

  header = "tree #{tree_content.bytesize}\0"
  store = header + tree_content
  sha = Digest::SHA1.hexdigest(store)
  FileUtils.mkdir_p(OBJECTS_DIR)
  File.binwrite(File.join(OBJECTS_DIR, sha), store)
  puts sha
end

def commit_tree(tree_sha, message)
  die "commit-tree requires tree SHA" unless tree_sha
  die "commit-tree requires -m message" unless message
  die "not a valid object name #{tree_sha}" unless File.exist?(File.join(OBJECTS_DIR, tree_sha))

  timestamp = Time.now.to_i
  author = "minigit <minigit@example.com> #{timestamp} +0000"
  committer = author
  content = "tree #{tree_sha}\n"
  content << "author #{author}\n"
  content << "committer #{committer}\n"
  content << "\n#{message}\n"

  header = "commit #{content.bytesize}\0"
  store = header + content
  sha = Digest::SHA1.hexdigest(store)
  FileUtils.mkdir_p(OBJECTS_DIR)
  File.binwrite(File.join(OBJECTS_DIR, sha), store)
  puts sha
end

def usage
  die "usage: minigit <init|hash-object|cat-file|update-index|write-tree|commit-tree>"
end

command = ARGV.shift
case command
when 'init'
  init
when 'hash-object'
  hash_object(ARGV.shift)
when 'cat-file'
  cat_file(ARGV.shift)
when 'update-index'
  update_index(*ARGV)
when 'write-tree'
  write_tree
when 'commit-tree'
  tree_sha = ARGV.shift
  message = nil
  while (arg = ARGV.shift)
    if arg == '-m'
      message = ARGV.shift
      break
    end
  end
  commit_tree(tree_sha, message)
else
  usage
end
```
